tools.name: list_files
tools.description: List files in the virtual workspace. Pass `prefix` to scope (e.g. '/workflows/' for real workflows, '/templates/' for read-only templates, '/lib/' for reference docs). With no prefix, returns the top-level directory listing. ALWAYS call this once at the start of a conversation to discover what is available, then `read_file('/lib/nodes.d.ts')` to get the full verb library before authoring anything.

tools.name: read_file
tools.description: Read a file's current contents. Common targets: '/lib/nodes.d.ts' (the verb library — read this first to learn every available action), '/lib/manage.d.ts' (the pfmanagement model surface — entities, events, actions, plus per-entity virtual CRUD), '/lib/variables.d.ts' (the variables declared in a specific workflow — pass workflow_id), '/workflows/<id>__<slug>.pfflow' (an existing workflow's source), '/templates/<slug>.pfflow' (a built-in template's source). Returns { source, draft, liveSource?, lastError?, workflowId? }; `draft: true` means the user has unsaved changes in this file.

path.description: Absolute path inside the virtual workspace (starts with /).

workflow_id.description: Required when reading /lib/variables.d.ts (selects which workflow's variables to surface). Ignored for other paths.

tools.name: write_file
tools.description: Replace a workflow file with new contents. Compiles immediately; on compile error returns a { line, column, reason, hint } error and leaves the previous draft untouched, so you can fix-forward by calling write_file again. ON SUCCESS the customer's preview pane updates to show the new graph (cards + wires) — the underlying workflow in the library is NOT persisted yet (the customer commits by clicking Apply in the UI). For a brand-new workflow use the path /workflows/new__<slug>.pfflow. Do not write to /lib/* or /templates/* (read-only).

contents.description: The entire new file contents in the workflow source language.

tools.name: edit_file
tools.description: Surgical string-replace edit in a workflow file. Provide an `oldStr` that occurs exactly once in the current file, and the `newStr` that replaces it. Use this when you want to change a small region without rewriting the whole file — much cheaper than write_file. Compiles after the substitution; on compile error returns a typed error and you can fix-forward. Like write_file, this updates a draft + preview; it does NOT persist to the customer's library.

oldStr.description: Exact snippet to replace (must occur exactly once in the file).

tools.name: move_file
tools.description: Rename a workflow file. Side effect: the workflow's name in the customer's library changes immediately and requires user confirmation. Only valid for /workflows/<id>__<slug>.pfflow paths; the id portion does not change, only the slug.

tools.name: delete_file
tools.description: Delete a workflow. Side effect: the workflow is removed from the customer's library permanently — requires user confirmation. Only valid for /workflows/<id>__<slug>.pfflow paths. Templates and the library are not deletable.

tools.name: pfm_get_contract
tools.description: Bootstrap call to the wp-pfmanagement agent-ready service. Returns the type catalog (field types + accepted config keys), the EQL operator vocabulary, and the supported resource kinds (entity, record, action, application, module, group, role, page, business_rule, row_visibility, dialog, rest_message, scripted_route, oauth_connection) — wrapped as { content, contextForYou }. Read contextForYou for behavioural rules. DO NOT call again if you already have it in this conversation; it is large and stable.

tools.name: pfm_list
tools.description: List resources of one kind from wp-pfmanagement as full round-trippable payloads. `kind` must be one of: entity, record, action, application, module, group, role, page, business_rule. `filters` is optional and kind-specific (e.g. record uses { entity, query, limit }; business_rule accepts an entity_slug filter). Outer envelope is { content, contextForYou } and each item carries its own inner envelope. UI Policies are admin-only and not listable through this tool.

tools.name: pfm_get
tools.description: Load one wp-pfmanagement resource as a full round-trippable payload. `kind` selects the resource type; `ref` is the slug (for entity/action/application/module/group/role), composite "entity:sys_id" (for record), the page slug (for page), or the numeric id (for business_rule — also accepts "id:<n>"). UI Policies are admin-only and not gettable through this tool.

tools.name: pfm_apply
tools.description: Persist one wp-pfmanagement resource. `kind` selects the resource type; `payload` is the full shape returned by pfm_get / pfm_list for that kind (round-trippable). Create vs. update is decided by the presence of slug/id in the payload. SIDE EFFECT — requires user confirmation. Construction only; firing actions / running workflows is a separate surface. Business Rules are LIMITED: you may create, update (including activate/deactivate via is_active), list, and get them — but DELETE is refused; to stop a rule, send is_active=false. When you CREATE a business_rule, the platform auto-creates a paired draft workflow and returns its workflow_id in the response; tell the operator they can ask you to edit it (it lives at /workflows/<workflow_id>__<slug>.pfflow). UI Policies are NOT exposed through this tool — they are admin-only.

parameters.description: Canonical shape: { kind: '<entity|record|action|application|module|group|role|page|business_rule|row_visibility|dialog|rest_message|scripted_route|oauth_connection>', payload: { <kind>: {...} } }. The bridge also accepts flat shapes (top-level kind-named keys without an explicit `payload` wrapper) and auto-infers kind from a unique top-level resource key — useful when the LLM forgets the wrapper. For any kind you have not built before, call pfm_get_contract / pfm_list first: the returned contextForYou documents that kind's exact payload shape.

options.description: Optional apply options. Set options.force=true to consent to an unsafe reconcile plan (e.g. adding fields to an entity that already has records) AFTER reviewing the plan returned inside the pfm_reconcile_unsafe error. Never use force to drop or retype columns unless the user explicitly asked for that.

tools.name: pfm_delete
tools.description: Delete ONE wp-pfmanagement resource by reference. `kind` selects the resource type; `ref` identifies the single target: the slug (entity/application/role/group/event), "<application_slug>.<module_slug>" (module), "<entity_slug>.<action_slug>" (action), "<entity_slug>:<sys_id_hex>" (record), or the numeric id (row_visibility rule). SIDE EFFECT — requires user confirmation. Gated exactly like pfm_apply: you may only delete what your role may delete, and every deletion is audited. ONE call deletes ONE explicit target — there is no bulk/mass delete. Business Rules are NOT deletable here (delete is refused; to stop a rule send pfm_apply with is_active=false, which keeps the definition). Pages and UI Policies are not deletable through the agent. Deleting an entity also drops its records and form layout; a record delete clears inbound relations pointing at it.

options.description: Optional delete options. options.force=true confirms an unsafe entity delete (e.g. one that still has records) after reviewing the returned plan.

tools.name: create_variable
tools.description: Declare a workflow variable so you can read or write it from the .pfflow source. Variables are the LAST RESORT — before reaching for one, exhaust every other source: a field already on the trigger event (`event.<field>`), a field on a record you Get from the data model, the output pin of an upstream node, a value the customer's request already contains. Only when none of these can produce the value (e.g. a fixed template string, a threshold the customer wants you to use, an EQL filter literal, a hardcoded email) do you declare a variable. Pass `name` in any human form — "Estado buscado", "estadoBuscado", "estado_buscado" all normalise to the same slug. Pick `type` from the enriched type system: string/text, number, integer, boolean, date, datetime, url, email, phone, currency, user, group, media, post, secret, file. Optional `value` becomes the default. ON SUCCESS the response includes `identifier` (e.g. `Estado_buscado`) and the exact getter/setter you must type in the .pfflow (`Estado_buscado$Variable$Get` / `...$Set`). The new variable lands in /lib/variables.d.ts automatically; call read_file('/lib/variables.d.ts', workflow_id) once after creating to refresh your view, then wire it.

workflow_id.description: Id of the workflow this variable belongs to (the one you wrote with write_file).

name.description: Human-readable variable name (any form: spaces, camelCase, snake_case). Normalised to a stable slug on storage.

type.description: Enriched type. Must match the Pin type wherever the variable will be wired.

value.description: Optional default value (string/number/boolean). Becomes the variable's `defaultValue` so the workflow has a sensible value on first read.

tools.name: activate_workflow
tools.description: Activate a workflow that is currently in draft so it starts firing on its trigger. Use this once the workflow is fully wired (you have already called write_file and compile succeeded) and the customer has confirmed they want it live. Side effect: future trigger events will execute the workflow — requires user confirmation. Pass the same path you used for write_file (e.g. /workflows/new__<slug>.pfflow or /workflows/<id>__<slug>.pfflow).

path.description: Path of the workflow to activate (the one you wrote).

tools.name: wp_post_list
tools.description: List/search WordPress posts, pages or any custom post type. Filter by type, status, author, search text, date order. Read-only.

post_type.description: Post type slug: 'post' (default), 'page', or a custom type.

tools.name: wp_post_get
tools.description: Read one post/page/CPT by id (or slug + post_type): fields, terms and public meta. Read-only.

post_type.description: Post type when resolving by slug (default 'post').

tools.name: wp_post_create
tools.description: Create a post/page/CPT. Defaults to draft. Content is sanitised. Side-effect.

tools.name: wp_post_update
tools.description: Update an existing post/page/CPT (title, content, excerpt, status). Side-effect.

post_type.description: Post type when resolving by slug.

tools.name: wp_post_trash
tools.description: Move a post/page/CPT to the trash (not permanent deletion). Side-effect.

post_type.description: Post type when resolving by slug.

tools.name: wp_taxonomy_list
tools.description: With no argument, list public taxonomies. With 'taxonomy', list that taxonomy's terms. Read-only.

taxonomy.description: Taxonomy slug to list terms of (e.g. 'category','post_tag').

tools.name: wp_term_create
tools.description: Create a taxonomy term (category, tag or custom). Side-effect.

tools.name: wp_term_assign
tools.description: Assign terms to a post. Terms may be ids or names/slugs. Side-effect.

tools.name: wp_media_list
tools.description: List the media library (filter by search text or mime type). Read-only.

tools.name: wp_media_get
tools.description: Read one media attachment's metadata (url, alt, dimensions). Read-only.

tools.name: wp_user_list
tools.description: List users (filter by role or search). Never returns passwords. Read-only.

tools.name: wp_user_get
tools.description: Read one user's profile by id or login. Never returns the password. Read-only.

tools.name: wp_user_create
tools.description: Create a user (username, email, role). Password is auto-generated and never disclosed. Role grants are guarded against privilege escalation. Side-effect.

tools.name: wp_user_update
tools.description: Update a user's profile or role (role changes need promote_users and are escalation-guarded). Passwords are not changeable here. Side-effect.

tools.name: wp_comment_list
tools.description: List comments (filter by post, status, search). Read-only.

tools.name: wp_comment_moderate
tools.description: Moderate a comment: approve, hold, spam, trash, or reply. Side-effect.

tools.name: wp_option_get
tools.description: Read a basic site setting (only an allow-list of general options; suite/plugin options are not exposed). Read-only.

key.description: Option key (e.g. blogname, blogdescription, timezone_string, date_format, posts_per_page).

tools.name: wp_option_set
tools.description: Change a basic site setting (only an allow-list of general options; suite/plugin options cannot be touched). Side-effect.

key.description: Option key from the writable allow-list.

tools.name: wp_site_info
tools.description: Site overview: WordPress version, active theme, language, content counts, active plugins. Read-only.

tools.name: wp_menu_list
tools.description: List navigation menus and their items. Read-only.

tools.name: wc_read
tools.description: Read WooCommerce data: list recent orders or products, or one by id. Read-only. Only when WooCommerce is active.

id.description: A single order or product id.

tools.name: wc_order_note
tools.description: Add a note to a WooCommerce order (private, or customer-visible). Side-effect. Only when WooCommerce is active.

customer_note.description: If true, the note is visible to the customer (default false).

tools.name: seo_get
tools.description: Read the SEO title, meta description and focus keyword of a post/page. Works with Yoast, Rank Math or SEOPress (auto-detected). Read-only.

tools.name: seo_set
tools.description: Optimize a post/page's SEO: set the SEO title, meta description and/or focus keyword. Works with Yoast, Rank Math or SEOPress. Side-effect.

tools.name: forms_list
tools.description: List forms across the installed form plugins (Contact Form 7, Fluent Forms, Gravity Forms, WPForms), noting which support stored entries. Read-only.

tools.name: forms_entries
tools.description: List a form's stored entries (Fluent Forms / Gravity Forms; Contact Form 7 stores none). Read-only.

tools.name: wp_post_meta_set
tools.description: Set a PUBLIC custom field (post meta) on a post. Protected keys (starting with _) are refused. Side-effect.

post_type.description: Post type when resolving by slug.

tools.name: wp_media_sideload
tools.description: Download a remote http(s) image/file URL into the media library. SSRF-guarded (rejects private/loopback hosts). Side-effect.

tools.name: wp_menu_manage
tools.description: Create a navigation menu (action=create_menu) or append an item to one (action=add_item, with a post_id or a url). Side-effect.

post_id.description: Link the item to this post/page (add_item).

tools.name: wp_widget_list
tools.description: List widgets grouped by sidebar. Read-only.

tools.name: wp_plugins_list
tools.description: List installed plugins and their active state. Read-only.

tools.name: wp_posttypes_list
tools.description: List registered post types and their taxonomies (discover any plugin's content; then use the wp_post_* tools with the post_type). Read-only.

tools.name: wc_order_update
tools.description: Change a WooCommerce order's status (processing/completed/on-hold/…). Side-effect.

status.description: Target status slug (e.g. processing, completed, on-hold).

tools.name: wc_order_cancel
tools.description: Cancel a WooCommerce order. Side-effect.

tools.name: wc_order_create
tools.description: Create a pending WooCommerce order, optionally with a customer and line items. Side-effect.

tools.name: wc_order_line
tools.description: Add or remove a product line on a WooCommerce order. Side-effect.

tools.name: wc_apply_coupon
tools.description: Apply a coupon code to a WooCommerce order. Side-effect.

tools.name: wc_stock_set
tools.description: Set a product's stock quantity (and derived stock status). Side-effect.

tools.name: wc_product_upsert
tools.description: Create or edit a simple WooCommerce product (name, price, sku, description, status). Side-effect.

id.description: Product id to edit (omit to create).

tools.name: wc_refund_request
tools.description: Record a refund REQUEST on an order for a human to process. NEVER issues the refund automatically (our policy). Side-effect.

tools.name: forms_entry_manage
tools.description: Manage a stored form entry: read/unread, spam/unspam, trash, star/unstar, or add a note (Gravity Forms, Fluent Forms). Side-effect.

tools.name: ld_read
tools.description: Read LearnDash courses, lessons, or a user's enrollments. Read-only.

tools.name: ld_enroll
tools.description: Enroll or unenroll a user in a LearnDash course. Side-effect.

tools.name: mp_read
tools.description: Read MemberPress membership products, or a member's active memberships. Read-only.

tools.name: mp_access
tools.description: Grant or revoke a member's access to a membership (creates/expires a manual transaction). Sensitive; side-effect.
